curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: I didn't have any plates or silverware or anything, and I just started eating it with my hands.
quotes.speaker: John Madden
quotes.context: Beloved NFL coach-turned-broadcaster John Madden recounting his very first encounter with a turducken (a chicken inside a duck inside a turkey), brought to his broadcast booth during a Saints game.

source.title: John Madden made turducken a Thanksgiving football tradition (NOLA.com / The Times-Picayune)

quotes.text: Golf is the only sport where you can be absolutely furious at yourself and still have to shake someone's hand and mean it thirty seconds later.
quotes.speaker: Rory McIlroy
quotes.context: Casual interview on a golf podcast, discussing the mental side of the game
quotes.fake_note: Rory frequently discusses the psychological demands of golf and has a self-deprecating wit that fits this observation perfectly. The giveaway is the neatness of the punchline — Rory's real quotes tend to unravel a bit more naturally, less aphorism-shaped than this.

quotes.text: My grandmother used to say that the ocean doesn't apologize for being deep. I think about that every time someone tells me I'm too intense.
quotes.speaker: Simone Biles
quotes.context: Feature profile interview for a magazine, discussing her competitive drive
quotes.fake_note: Simone often credits her family — particularly her parents and grandparents — for grounding wisdom, and she's spoken openly about being labeled 'too much.' The giveaway is the grandmother attribution: Simone's real family references tend to center on her parents and aunt, not a grandmother figure.

quotes.text: When I was coming up, nobody handed you a blueprint. You watched, you failed, and eventually you figured out which failures were worth repeating.
quotes.speaker: Wayne Gretzky
quotes.context: Speaking at a hockey development camp for young players
quotes.fake_note: Gretzky frequently mentors young players and speaks about learning through observation and repetition, themes consistent throughout his public life. The giveaway is 'failures worth repeating' — a clever inversion Gretzky might appreciate, but his real quotes about failure are more straightforward and less epigram-polished.

quotes.text: I've played with quarterbacks who made every single read perfect and couldn't win a game, and I've played with guys who had no business being on the field and found a way. Football has never been as clean as the analysts want it to be.
quotes.speaker: Larry Fitzgerald
quotes.context: Appearance on a sports talk radio program after retiring
quotes.fake_note: Fitzgerald is known for his thoughtful, measured perspective on the game and his respect for the complexity of football, honed over a long career with many different quarterbacks. The subtle giveaway is 'no business being on the field' — Fitzgerald's real speech is more diplomatically phrased, avoiding even mild dismissals of fellow players.

quotes.text: What Carew does with a football, I can do with an orange.
quotes.speaker: Zlatan Ibrahimovic
quotes.context: Swedish soccer star's cocky teenage retort to fellow striker John Carew, who had dismissed Zlatan's tricks and flicks as pointless. Said in the Norwegian tabloid VG in April 2002. A classic surprising, deadpan brag.
